critiques.why: Targets a real weakness, but the +11pp math assumes the 12 regressors are real at n=3/skill and that boundaries flip exactly one loss each. In a matched skill-vs-placebo eval, when-NOT-to-use text may not fire and can consume useful prompt budget.

critiques.hypothesis: Agent-native executable checklists for procedural skills
critiques.why: Most mechanism-aligned: procedural skills were the only class where full guides beat triggers, and no-fabrication/stop rules address observed harms. Risk: if applied indiscriminately to conceptual skills it becomes template theater.

critiques.hypothesis: Hybrid injection: compact conceptual triggers, full procedural checklists
critiques.why: Supported by the trigger-vs-instruction probe and directly attacks verbosity. Needs pre-registered classification plus fresh holdout; otherwise it is cherry-picking from a tiny n=8 probe.

critiques.hypothesis: Router harness selects zero or one skill and blocks overlaps
critiques.why: Could help real deployment, but current behavioral eval mostly tests matched skill problems, not false-positive routing. Any lift depends on changing the task distribution and denominator.
Likely to improve judge-facing structure more than correctness. A single example per skill can teach to the benchmark or Gemini's preferences, especially if examples resemble eval tasks.

critiques.hypothesis: Mandatory WHEN NOT TO USE/failure modes atop all skills
critiques.why: Directionally sensible, but confidence is overstated. Anti-triggers alone do not teach better execution on in-scope problems and can create false abstentions.

critiques.hypothesis: Truncate all conceptual skills to 2-sentence triggers
critiques.why: Promising subset idea, but the evidence is only an 8-skill probe and shorter prompts change both length and attention allocation. Needs same-length trigger-placebo controls.

critiques.hypothesis: Strip human-centric verbs and replace with agent primitives
critiques.why: Necessary hygiene for flagged skills, but subset effect is bounded: six skills cannot plausibly deliver +10pp aggregate unless many current losses are concentrated there and reliably flip.

critiques.hypothesis: Rewrite all skills as dense pseudocode/YAML capped at 300 words
critiques.why: Format may bias judges toward structured answers without improving reasoning. Also confounds density, length, and instruction style at once.
Overlap exists, but strict routing is already fairly high and broad meta-skills may dilute specificity. Also changes the unit of evaluation, making win-rate comparisons unstable.
Classic selection-on-noise: n=3 per skill makes regressor/proven labels unreliable. Reusing the same data to prune losers will show apparent lift that regresses on holdout.

critiques.hypothesis: Stop conditions plus strip human-only directions
critiques.why: Plausible quality fix, but its own conservative estimate is below +10pp. It mainly helps false-positive deployment, not necessarily matched behavioral comparisons.

critiques.hypothesis: Bifurcate collection by trigger-probe result after probing all skills
critiques.why: Best version of the trigger hypothesis because it proposes measuring all skills first. Still must lock decisions before a fresh holdout or it becomes overfit model selection.

critiques.hypothesis: Consolidate unanimous overlap clusters and sharpen router descriptions
critiques.why: Likely positive but too small: the proposed mechanism predicts about +5 wins, not the +11 wins needed for a 10pp aggregate lift.
Targets the deepest causal critique: human artifacts ported to agents. Could produce real lift if paired with executable procedures, but examples alone risk becoming another taxonomy.

critiques.hypothesis: Capability gate: skip skills when direct reasoning suffices
critiques.why: The gate requires an oracle: knowing direct reasoning suffices is nearly equivalent to solving the task. It also changes the eval from skill-vs-placebo to selective abstention.

ranked_top3: Agent-native executable checklists with calibration/no-fabrication guards, especially for procedural and leakage-prone skills.
Pre-registered hybrid injection: compact trigger cards for conceptual skills, full checklists only for procedural skills, tested on fresh holdout.
LLM-native rewrite targeting sycophancy, over-framing, hallucinated evidence, and verification failures rather than human cognitive biases.
most_likely_mirage: Removing the 12 regressing skills: it will look great on the existing table because it selects losers after observing noisy n=3 outcomes, but that is winner's-curse pruning, not evidence of durable lift.
missing_hypothesis: Add an independent post-skill verifier/reversion gate: after a skill drafts an answer, a separate no-framework pass checks task correctness, unsupported assumptions, fabricated facts, and whether the direct answer would be better; if so, discard the framework output. This targets losses from framework theater without relying on an oracle router.
